subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear organization with a discernible introduction, body and conclusion
Logical progression of sections aided by headings, numbered subsections and a contents page
Generally relevant content with concise mathematical steps linking between sections
weaknesses: Typographical glitches and duplicated phrases intermittently disrupt flow
Mis-aligned tables and page fragments reduce polish
Extended verbatim derivations and definition boxes add unnecessary length
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the exploration at band 3 for coherence and structure, and band 2 for conciseness. The logical development and clear structure justify a mark of 3 out of 4, but typographical errors and some digressions prevent the highest band.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, diagrams, GeoGebra screenshots) to model the guitar outline
Variables are defined and equations are presented in mathematical form
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (decimal separators, subscripts) and LaTeX breaks
Long blocks of algebraic working lack commentary, leading to unexplained jumps
Missing units and integration limits diminish clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With strand scores of 2 for language, 3 for representation and 2 for structure, the overall performance is moderate. The variety of representations is a strength but inconsistent notation and gaps in explanation limit the band to 2 out of 4.

strengths: Personal connection through use of the studentâs own guitar and bespoke point selection
Some creativity shown via a custom âoperation countâ metric and golden-ratio redesign
Evidence of independent thinking in method comparison and sustainability linkage
weaknesses: Approach remains fairly straightforward and occasionally superficial
Innovations like the custom metric are not fully developed or justified
Limited depth in linking aesthetic modelling choices to research rationale
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands scored band 2 (significant independent thinking, clear personal approach, good creativity). This warrants 2 out of 3âsolid personal engagement without the exceptional innovation of the top band.

strengths: Meaningful reflections on unexpected results and sustainability implications
Thoughtful analysis comparing accuracy versus computational effort
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for improvement
weaknesses: Reflections are brief and not deeply integrated throughout the exploration
Limited discussion of measurement uncertainty and error propagation
Evaluation lacks forward-looking implications and broader context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets band 2 criteria for reflection, with meaningful but not substantial or deeply critical analysis. The level of insight justifies 2 out of 3.

strengths: Use of integration, linear algebra, regression and matrix methods appropriate for SL level
Varied mathematical techniques are applied coherently to model the guitar outline
Generally sound understanding of concepts, with correct methodology for main calculations
weaknesses: Calculation errors (sign slips, mis-typed decimals) and repeated limit misassignments reduce accuracy
Some steps are copied or applied mechanically without full justification
Error analysis and uncertainty propagation are omitted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands scored band 4 for relevance and level-appropriateness but band 3 for understanding/accuracy. The mixture of effective SL-level techniques with recurring calculation mistakes places this criterion at 3 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are mostly logical, yet long blocks of working are inserted without commentary, and some jumps (e.g., derivation of the operations count formula) are unexplained.  Clarity is therefore moderate rather than good.

Working suggests sound understanding; the area calculations and least-squares outputs are plausible, but calculation errors (wrong slope for fâ, sign slips, mis-typed decimals) and algebraic inconsistencies occur often enough to lower confidence.

The student employs tables, labelled diagrams and GeoGebra screenshots together with algebraic expressions to represent the guitar outline effectively.  These multiple representations support understanding, though axes and scales are not always fully explained.

The report follows a clear line from introduction â methodology â results â reflection, and each mathematical step generally leads on from the previous one.  Nevertheless, frequent typographical glitches, duplicated phrases, and some digressions (e.g., long quotations of definitions) occasionally interrupt the flow, so coherence is good rather than exemplary.

Introducing a homemade âoperation countâ efficiency metric and extending the task to a golden-ratio redesign indicates some creativity, but implementation remains fairly straightforward and occasionally superficial.

There is thoughtful analysis comparing accuracy versus computational effort and discussing limitations such as measurement error; still, the analysis stops short of being âdeep and insightfulâ across the full investigation.

Outcomes are evaluated (e.g., 1 % area reduction, ineffectiveness ratio) and possible improvements are listed in a table.  This is a clear evaluation but not comprehensive or forward-looking enough for the top band.
The student comments on why results were unexpected and mentions sustainability, providing meaningful reflection, yet these insights are brief and not deeply integrated across the work.

Variables are defined and equations written in mathematical form, but LaTeX breaks, inconsistent decimal separators and mismatched subscripts appear throughout, so usage is only sometimes appropriate.

The exploration is clearly centred on the studentâs own instrument, and choices such as point selection and scaling factor constitute a personal approach; however these touches are not sufficiently innovative to merit the top band.

Most material is relevant, yet extended derivations are reproduced verbatim for every linear segment, and several definition boxes add little to the investigation.  These repetitions make the exploration noticeably longer than necessary.
Integration, linear functions, polynomial regression, logarithms and matrix least-squares are all pertinent to modelling the guitar outline, showing effective and varied use of mathematics.

Selecting a personal guitar, deciding to compare two approximation methods and linking the outcome to sustainability shows noticeable independent thinking, but the mathematics largely follows standard procedures from sources.

Headings, numbered subsections, tables and figures give the exploration a recognisable structure with introduction, body, conclusion and evaluation.  However, page fragments and mis-aligned tables lessen the overall polish, preventing a level-4 impression.

Most techniques (integration, linear algebra) are at or slightly above SL level but still appropriate and applied reasonably.  Some matrix manipulation steps are copied mechanically, preventing a higher band.

annotations.comment: The table of contents effectively maps the structure, demonstrating clear organization. Consider removing repeated ellipses to enhance professionalism.
Some page number entries appear misaligned and inconsistent; ensure uniform tab stops in the contents for improved clarity.
Definition of the golden ratio uses inline math but lacks proper formatting; rewrite as $$\phi \approx 1.618$$ to ensure consistent mathematical notation.
The discussion of nonlinear regression conflates curved models with sum of squares; refine language to distinguish between regression type and minimization objective.
The preformatted code block for variable definitions disrupts flow; replace with inline math and list formatting to improve readability.
The definite integral shows the concept, but missing integration limits in text; explicitly define $$a$$ and $$b$$ limits for clarity.
The introduction begins with extended background on sustainability; trim non-essential content to improve conciseness and maintain focus on the research aim.
The rationale for method selection is personal and engaging, but the justification would benefit from referencing specific source limitations to deepen critical analysis.
The linear function formula is presented correctly; consider adding labels to clarify that $$a$$ is slope and $$b$$ is intercept for consistent notation.
Polynomial regression equation is well formatted; however, ensure consistent vector notation for parameters: $$\beta_0,\beta_1,\dots,\beta_n$$.
Table 1 repeats its header and includes empty rows, reducing conciseness; streamline table entries and remove redundant information.
The description of choosing 15 segments lacks rationale; explain choice of segmentation to demonstrate critical planning.
The multi-block equation for functions is overly dense; split derivations for each function into separate, labelled subsections to aid clarity.
The composite integral expression repeats $$p_1$$ limits incorrectly; verify domain indices to ensure accurate computation.
Result is stated without unit consistency; include units in numerical results (e.g., $$2323.3\ \mathrm{cm}^2$$) for precision.
Introducing the operations count metric is innovative, but the formula is not fully explained; define each term to strengthen justification.
The total operation count formula lacks clarity on summation indices; rewrite using sigma notation to improve mathematical communication.
Percentage ineffectiveness calculation is valid but stops at few significant figures; expand to discuss implications in context to add depth to evaluation.
The golden ratio modelling is well positioned but would benefit from an introductory rationale linking aesthetics to sustainability to increase engagement.
The mapping process includes uncertainty but omits propagation; incorporate error analysis for measurement uncertainties to strengthen mathematical accuracy.
Figure 1 effectively illustrates the guitar but lacks axis labels and scale indicators; add annotations to improve interpretation.
When deriving $$f(x)=ax+b$$, clarify the transition steps explicitly to avoid unexplained jumps in reasoning.
Slope calculation for $$f_1$$ is correct, though b is assumed zero; explicitly justify intercept choice to affirm understanding.
The scaling factor formula is mathematically appropriate; include a brief derivation to demonstrate understanding of its origin.
Difference in surface area is quantified but lacks reflection on measurement error; discuss potential uncertainty to deepen analysis.
The conclusion highlights surprising findings but should explicitly link back to the initial aim to reinforce overall coherence.
Bibliography includes relevant sources, but referencing style is inconsistent; adopt a uniform citation format for all entries.
Bibliography header is clear but author names and retrieval details lack consistency; revise to maintain uniform formatting throughout.
